steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice describes table data that support Persad's conclusion. The conclusion has two parts: (1) concentrated precipitation triggers more irrigation, and (2) this effect is “highly sensitive to the baseline concentration.” A supporting choice must show that the irrigation increase is noticeably different depending on whether baseline precipitation is already somewhat concentrated or evenly distributed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — If baseline precipitation is somewhat concentrated, the amount of water being used for irrigation will increase 0.4% for surface water and 0.9% for groundwater, whereas the amount of water entering aquifers will increase 11.0% if baseline precipitation is evenly distributed.: mixes aquifer data with irrigation data and misattributes the 11.0% figure. The conclusion is about irrigation sensitivity, not aquifer recharge. ✗
  • Choice B — If baseline precipitation is somewhat concentrated, water use for irrigation will increase only slightly, whereas it will increase 9.0% for surface water and 7.9% for groundwater if baseline precipitation is evenly distributed.: directly shows that irrigation response is large when the baseline is evenly distributed but small when it is already somewhat concentrated — exactly the “highly sensitive to baseline” claim. ✓
  • Choice C — If baseline precipitation is somewhat concentrated, the amount of water entering aquifers will increase 4.9%, while the amount being used for irrigation will increase 0.4% for surface water and 0.9% for groundwater.: focuses on aquifer recharge, not irrigation. Does not address the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice D — If baseline precipitation is somewhat concentrated, water use for irrigation will decline by a small amount, whereas it will increase 11.0% for surface water and 9.0% for groundwater if baseline precipitation is evenly distributed.: contradicts the conclusion that concentrated precipitation triggers more irrigation. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
